title: Clickable links in Monday automation emails not working in Outlook Desktop – any solution?

posts.author: c.lacor (Christelle LACOR)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently facing an issue with email automations in Monday.
When I send an email via a standard Monday automation (not the Outlook integration), the URL included in the email is clickable in Gmail, but not clickable at all in Outlook Desktop.

I’ve already tried several things:

  • using a Link column

  • using a plain text URL

  • isolating the URL with blank lines before and after

  • wrapping the URL in < >

  • sending the URL in its raw form

  • even different URL formats

Nothing seems to make Outlook Desktop turn the URL into a clickable link.
Gmail works perfectly, but Outlook Desktop doesn’t.

Has anyone found a reliable workaround or configuration that makes links clickable in Outlook Desktop when sending emails through Monday’s default automations?

Thanks a lot for your help!

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @c.lacor if you are open to use a marketplace app, SuperMail lets you design and send emails with properly formatted HTML links that render correctly across email clients including Outlook. You can check it out here: SuperMail – Email Automation - monday.com Marketplace — and this guide covers how it works: https://spot-nik.com/how-to-use-supermail/

posts.author: c.lacor (Christelle LACOR)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for your recommandation. I’m looking for a solution free of charge

Hey @c.lacor !

This is a known limitation with how monday sends emails and how Outlook Desktop parses them.

Outlook Desktop is stricter with link formatting and often won’t recognize plain text URLs from automated emails as clickable, even if Gmail does.

There isn’t a reliable way to force plain text links to become clickable in Outlook using monday’s standard email automations.

A couple of workarounds that tend to help:

  • Use a Link column and insert it via a button or formatted field (sometimes Outlook handles structured links better than raw text, though not always consistent)

  • Keep the URL very clean (no line breaks, no extra characters), as Outlook can break detection easily

  • If clickable links are critical, some teams switch to sending emails through Outlook or Gmail integrations instead of the default monday automation, since those use the mail client’s formatting

If none of those solve it in your case, it’s likely due to how Outlook Desktop renders these emails rather than something configurable on the monday side. In that case, sharing an example with support can help confirm if there’s any recent change or workaround available.
